name: Housing Development Corporation Of Northeast
description_short: Non-profit homebuyer assistance organization providing free counseling and closing cost help to low-to-moderate income residents of Luzerne County, PA.
description_long:
Housing Development Corporation of Northeast (HDC) is a non-profit organization based in Luzerne County, Pennsylvania, with offices in Wilkes-Barre and Hazleton. The organization was established to help individuals and families achieve homeownership by removing financial and educational barriers that prevent low-to-moderate income households from purchasing homes.

HDC offers two primary services: free homebuyer counseling and closing cost assistance grants. The free counseling helps prospective homebuyers understand the homeownership process, while the closing cost assistance program provides up to $7,500 in direct financial aid. Eligibility is based on household income thresholds that vary by family size, ranging from $49,400 for a single-person household to $93,200 for an eight-person household. The program is available exclusively to Luzerne County residents.

HDC distinguishes itself through its HUD alignment and zero-cost educational services. The organization operates under HUD guidelines and Equal Housing Opportunity standards, indicating compliance with federal homeownership assistance protocols. All homebuyer counseling is provided at no charge, and the closing cost grants (up to $7,500) directly reduce financial barriers to homeownership without creating debt obligations.

The organization's scope is geographically limited to Luzerne County and focuses exclusively on first-time or aspiring homebuyers meeting income criteria. Prospective clients must contact the organization directly or initiate the application process online to determine eligibility and receive services. While comprehensive within its mission, HDC does not offer credit repair, loan origination, or post-purchase homeowner services.
pros: Free homebuyer counseling with no fees or hidden costs
Up to $7,500 in closing cost assistance grants (non-repayable)
HUD-approved organization operating under federal homeownership standards
Two office locations in Wilkes-Barre and Hazleton for accessibility
Clear income eligibility thresholds published online for self-assessment
Focus on low-to-moderate income households traditionally excluded from homeownership
Online application process available without requiring in-person visit initially
cons: Geographic service limitation to Luzerne County only—no assistance for residents elsewhere in Pennsylvania or other states
Maximum closing cost assistance of $7,500 may be insufficient in high-cost markets
Website lacks specific information about counseling duration, curriculum, or post-purchase support services
Income limits exclude households earning above specified thresholds with no alternative programs mentioned
No indication of application processing timeline or approval rates
best_for: Low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers in Luzerne County seeking free education before applying for mortgages
Families with children who meet income requirements and need assistance covering closing costs
Residents of Wilkes-Barre and Hazleton areas looking for accessible, local homeownership guidance
diagnosis: Best for low-to-moderate income Luzerne County residents who are first-time homebuyers and need free counseling plus closing cost assistance. Primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Luzerne County; this organization cannot help prospective homebuyers in other Pennsylvania regions or states.
services: Job placement opportunities through the organization
Referrals to Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA) resources
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 163 Amber Ln, Wilkes-Barre, PA 18702-6545, USA
